name: Knight Relay Chess
author: Mannis Charosh (implemented for AGP)
description: Chess where any piece (except a king or knight) defended by a friendly knight gains the power to also move and capture like a knight. Knights themselves cannot capture and cannot be captured -- they only relay their leaping power and act as immovable blockers. A knight cannot give check by itself, but a guarded piece can relay a knight-check. No en passant; pawns promote only by an ordinary pawn move. By Mannis Charosh (c. 1972). White (player 0) moves first.
